title: MD
biography: James Lind was a Scottish physician who conducted one of the first clinical trials in medical history in 1747, demonstrating that citrus fruits could cure and prevent scurvy. His controlled experiment on 12 sailors laid the groundwork for evidence-based medicine and clinical trial methodology.
aiSummary: James Lind was a medicine specialist who conducted one of the first clinical trials in 1747, proving citrus prevents scurvy. Pioneer of evidence-based medicine. Based in Edinburgh, United Kingdom.
awards: James Lind Library (named in his honor)

timeline.title: Conducted first controlled clinical trial
timeline.description: Tested six different scurvy treatments on 12 sailors aboard HMS Salisbury, proving citrus effective

timeline.title: Published 'A Treatise of the Scurvy'
timeline.description: Published the results of his clinical trial, one of the most important medical texts in history

timeline.title: Royal Navy adopted citrus rations
timeline.description: Decades after Lind's findings, the Royal Navy mandated lime juice for all sailors, eliminating scurvy
